<h2 class="postHeaderTitleText wp-block-post-title">La Puerta Falsa: Famous Restaurant In Bogota</h2>


<div style="height:30px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<p><strong>La Puerta Falsa (Calle 11 No 6-50), in<a data-lasso-id="71753" href="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/travel-to-colombia/destinations-in-colombia/bogota-around/bogota/" data-type="URL" data-id="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/travel-to-colombia/destinations-in-colombia/bogota-around/bogota/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> Bogota</a>’s historic La Candelaria District</strong>, is one of the oldest and most traditional restaurants in the city:<strong> it dates back to 1816</strong>, and, without wanting to make the place sound too old-fashioned, you can definitely tell! Located just off the Plaza Bolivar, in the main tourist hub of the city,<strong> </strong>La Puerta Falsa always has a long line of travelers wanting to verify <a data-lasso-id="71754" href="https://achefstour.com/blog/follow-in-the-footsteps-of-anthony-bourdain-in-colombia" data-type="URL" data-id="https://achefstour.com/blog/follow-in-the-footsteps-of-anthony-bourdain-in-colombia" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Anthony Bourdain’s claims</a> for themselves, plus a regular and loyal clientele who seem to come back time and again.</p>


<div class="gb-container gb-container-8b126dfe postAdvice left">
<div class="gb-container gb-container-3444d2d0 postAdviceContent" data-rocket-lazy-bg-4d0cc95f-7c76-400d-abc0-c83258664c58="loaded">
<div class="gb-container gb-container-b823a3af postAdviceContentBloc">

Quickly decide <strong><a href="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/ideal-district-hostel-bogota/" data-type="post" data-id="4851" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="93573">where to stay in the Colombian capital </a></strong>(+ a list <a style="font-weight: bold;" href="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/best-hotels-bogota-colombia/" data-type="post" data-id="43571" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="93574">of our favorite hotels</a>)</p>

</div>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" style="text-transform:capitalize"><strong>What Can You Eat at la Puerta Falsa</strong></h2>



<div class="wp-block-columns is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-9d6595d7 wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<figure class="wp-block-image size-full"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="768" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20768'%3E%3C/svg%3E" alt="Ajiaco colombia" class="wp-image-72455" data-lazy-srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia.jpg 1024w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia-300x225.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia-768x576.jpg 768w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" data-lazy-src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia.jpg"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="768" src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia.jpg" alt="Ajiaco colombia" class="wp-image-72455" srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia.jpg 1024w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia-300x225.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Ajiaco-colombia-768x576.jpg 768w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" /></noscript></figure>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<figure class="wp-block-image size-full"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="768" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20768'%3E%3C/svg%3E" alt="Hot chocolate and cheese Puerta Falsa" class="wp-image-72456" data-lazy-srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa.jpg 1024w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa-300x225.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa-768x576.jpg 768w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" data-lazy-src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa.jpg"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="768" src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa.jpg" alt="Hot chocolate and cheese Puerta Falsa" class="wp-image-72456" srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa.jpg 1024w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa-300x225.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Hot-chocolate-and-cheese-Puerta-Falsa-768x576.jpg 768w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" /></noscript></figure>
</div>
</div>



<p>La Puerta Falsa is the place to go in Bogota for delicious, traditional Colombian food. </p>



<p><strong>What especially stands out are the </strong><em><strong>ajiaco</strong> </em>(a hearty soup of potatoes, chicken, cream, and capers) <strong>and the </strong><em><strong>tamales</strong> </em>(which some claim are the best anywhere in the capital). It’s also the place to visit for an unusual Cundinamarca specialty: <strong>hot chocolate with cheese.</strong> Sounds weird, right!? However, in the hands of La Puerta Falsa, it’s a delight: rich, steamy chocolate combined with thick, gooey cheese makes for <strong>a heart-warming combo on a cold Bogota day.</strong></p>



<p>With its wood-paneled walls and 1800s atmosphere, the place radiates old-world charm. It’s cozy and a bit tight, ideal for a small, intimate meal. </p>



<div style="height:20px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading" style="text-transform:capitalize"><strong>Tips for La Puerta Falsa</strong></h2>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="746" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20746'%3E%3C/svg%3E" alt="Credit La puerta Falsa restaurant Bogota" class="wp-image-72555" data-lazy-srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-1024x746.jpg 1024w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-300x219.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-768x560.jpg 768w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota.jpg 1054w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" data-lazy-src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-1024x746.jpg"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="746" src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-1024x746.jpg" alt="Credit La puerta Falsa restaurant Bogota" class="wp-image-72555" srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-1024x746.jpg 1024w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-300x219.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota-768x560.jpg 768w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/Credit-La-puerta-Falsa-restaurant-Bogota.jpg 1054w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" /></noscript><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Tamal – Credit: <a href="https://www.instagram.com/lapuertafalsa1816/" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="72006">La Puerta Falsa</a></figcaption></figure>



<div style="height:10px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Arrive early or embrace the line – it’s worth the wait.</li>



<li>Must-try dishes: tamal, Hot chocolate with cheese, and sopa de ajíaco.</li>



<li>Open at 8 a.m. (instead of 7h30), so plan accordingly for early flights.</li>



<li>For quicker seating, split your group into smaller parties.</li>



<li>Don’t forget to ask for the homemade salsa picante.</li>
</ul>



<div style="height:20px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Alternative to La Puerta Falsa</h2>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-full"><img decoding="async" width="800" height="314" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20314'%3E%3C/svg%3E" alt="La puerta Falsa" class="wp-image-72454" data-lazy-srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa.jpg 800w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa-300x118.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa-768x301.jpg 768w" data-lazy-sizes="(max-width: 800px) 100vw, 800px" data-lazy-src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa.jpg"><noscript><img decoding="async" width="800" height="314" src="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa.jpg" alt="La puerta Falsa" class="wp-image-72454" srcset="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa.jpg 800w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa-300x118.jpg 300w, https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/La-puerta-Falsa-768x301.jpg 768w" sizes="(max-width: 800px) 100vw, 800px" /></noscript></figure>



<div style="height:20px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<p>When I visited La Puerta Falsa for the 2nd time, its ajiaco didn’t impress me. </p>



<p>Fortunately, Bogotá has other options for Colombian cuisine. Just next door, there’s a restaurant with similar dishes and prices. The name? <strong><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/1TGogkZCW6ySdiKM9" data-type="link" data-id="https://maps.app.goo.gl/1TGogkZCW6ySdiKM9" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="93575">El Mejor Ajiaco del Mundo</a></strong>—clearly, the owner is a marketing whiz.  Another option (also next to it) is the <strong><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/SDmg1o8Rr33McyGe8" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="93576">La Puerta de la Catedrale.</a></strong></p>



<p>When he was in Bogota, Bourdain stopped by to <strong><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/MJQatrMdWdoG6Yh67" data-lasso-id="93577" target="_blank" rel="noopener">see Señora Luz</a></strong>. She’s based in the <strong><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/4knCjj8xZ1sZ3kSM6" data-lasso-id="93578" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Perseverencia Market</a></strong>, which is a good alternative to <strong><a href="https://www.tomplanmytrip.com/paloquemao-market-bogota-colombia/" target="_blank" data-type="post" data-id="72827" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="93579">Paloquemao Market</a></strong> if you’re looking to taste exotic fruits. Señora Luz also offers Ajiaco.</p>



<p>For a delicious Ajiaco in a <strong>more elegant</strong> (and consequently more costly) restaurant, then head to <strong><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/TpixVTTyTz4GsiGN7" target="_blank" rel="noopener" data-lasso-id="93580">Santa Fe</a>.</strong></p>

</div>
</div>

<h1 class="wp-block-heading destTitle has-global-color-14-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-cc0e2ad84fa912cf7a1dfe40f66127cc"><strong>Your local travel agency in Colombia</strong></h1>



<p class="destInspirationInfos has-global-color-14-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-68d7a096b22c81a412b7451faa169604"><strong>Want to discover the best of Colombia without rushing around or overcomplicating things?</strong><br><br>We’ve spent <strong>11 years</strong> solving the perfect trip equation: maximize jaw-dropping experiences, eliminate unnecessary transfers, and avoid tourist hordes.</p>



<p class="destInspirationInfos has-global-color-14-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-111c8ca6b3661b7a5a165d4258050aa5"><strong>The result? </strong>The Colombia we fell in love with—not the cookie-cutter routes designed for everyone else—savored at a pace that lets you fully live each moment. No more superficial, exhausting trips.<br></p>

  <h3 class="wp-block-heading has-global-color-11-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-2f91d800425f787f57a7c59b91fe7e06">Bogotá</h3>
</div>
<div class="destMapInfos"><div>Located in the middle of the Eastern Andes mountain range and South America's third highest capital (over 2,600 m above sea level), Bogotá is a huge, bustling metropolis brimming with great experiences to discover Colombian culture and gastronomy. The hub of many of the region's tourist attractions, its airport makes it easy to get around the country.<br></div></div>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading destMoreTitle">Our secret to actually enjoying your Colombia vacation</h2>



<p class="destMoreInfos">Colombia is twice the size of France. There’s no point trying to see it all in 15 days. Our trips distill 11 years of boots-on-the-ground exploration into the essentials: the very best of the country, without the stress of racing around.</p>

metadata.description: Travel guides often talk about La Puerta Falsa. It’s true that it's a well-known restaurant in Bogota that was once very trendy. But is it still worth it today?
